factors.name: Semiconductor earnings disappointment
factors.summary: Broadcom set to shed $300B in value as AI results fail to impress; Micron flirts with record market-cap wipeout as Broadcom casts shadow over chip stocks. This is a major NDX headwind given heavy semiconductor weighting.

factors.name: Tech megacap selloff signals
factors.summary: Netflix down 24% since last earnings amid competitive pressures; Apple Services growth concerns noted by UBS; CrowdStrike and Broadcom underperforming expectations. Single-name weakness in large NDX constituents.

factors.name: Fed hawkish inflation messaging
factors.summary: Fed's Schmid calls inflation 'biggest risk' to US economy; persistent inflation narrative with 5-year fight ongoing. Daly confirms inflation remains top priority. This pressures long-duration growth valuations in NDX.

factors.name: Rotation into value/financials
factors.summary: Dow Jones surges 1.84% led by financials (GS +4.93%, JPM +3.64%, AXP +4.41%) — clear rotation OUT of tech into real economy. This suggests near-term NDX underperformance vs. broader market.

factors.name: Geopolitical relief narrative
factors.summary: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, Trump peace deal optimism, Putin signals willingness for Ukraine compromise. De-risking momentum supports equity appetite and lowers safe-haven USD bid, neutral-to-bullish for risk assets.

factors.name: IPO momentum and capital rotation
factors.summary: Quantinuum +13% above IPO, Innio +15%, Liftoff +21%, Sunshine Silver +11% — strong IPO demand signals investor appetite. However, crypto weakness (BTC below $62K) and Saylor's Strategy paper loss ($11B) suggest capital rotating FROM speculative tech TO IPO opportunities.

factors.name: Real yields / Treasury curve stress
factors.summary: BOE Bailey warns of market stress and leverage risks; UK 10Y up 97bps (4.23% to 5.20%), US 10Y up 76bps (3.92% to 4.687%). Rising real yields compress NDX multiples; war-weary Treasury market losing patience per MarketWatch.

factors.name: Labor market resilience mixed signals
factors.summary: Long-term unemployment surging per 2026 data; Treas Sec Bessent hints at strong tomorrow's jobs report ('I wish job market had come out today') — suggests strong NFP may be priced in but also fuels Fed hawkish lean.

factors.name: AI capex spending thesis intact but questioned
factors.summary: AI adoption 'incredibly shallow' per Bloomberg; Wasted AI budgets at Nvidia, Uber, Microsoft noted as 'tokenmaxxing' reversed. Job market benefiting as human workers cheaper than AI. Cracks in hyperscaler AI ROI narrative.

factors.name: Price action: modest 4h and 24h gains already booked
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.31% in 4h, +0.64% in 24h — very modest moves despite mixed news. Suggests market in digestion phase; no major breakout, but also no major breakdown yet.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught between competing forces: strong earnings misses in semiconductors (Broadcom, Micron), persistent Fed hawkish inflation messaging, and rising real yields are pressuring long-duration tech multiples and triggering rotation into value/financials (Dow +1.84%). Conversely, geopolitical de-risking (Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, Ukraine peace optimism), IPO appetite, and intact (if questioned) AI capex thesis provide some support. The market appears to be in a rebalancing phase: mega-cap tech valuations are being re-tested, and the narrative has shifted from 'all-in AI' to 'AI adoption shallow, capex efficiency under scrutiny.' Modest 4h/24h price action suggests consolidation ahead of Friday's NFP report, which will likely re-anchor Fed expectations.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (Non-Farm Payroll) Friday 2026-06-05 — critical for Fed rate expectations; Bessent's hint suggests strong beat likely
FOMC meeting minutes and next policy decision (timing TBD but likely in June cycle)
Earnings from mega-cap tech: AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA, TSLA — forward guidance on capex and cloud spending
10-year Treasury yield trajectory — if continues above 4.70%, real yields remain headwind for NDX
Semiconductor supply chain updates (TSMC, ASML, Samsung) following Broadcom/Micron weakness
China-Taiwan tensions and AI chip export control announcements
screening.risk_warning: If NFP Friday comes in materially cooler than expected (sub-150K print), Fed pivot to easing could reverse the bearish real-yield pressure and reignite tech/growth rally. Conversely, if NFP accelerates inflation trajectory, Fed hawkish hold could extend NDX selloff. Mega-cap earnings (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL) with upside guidance and strong capex outlooks would also invalidate the bearish semiconductor/capex ROI narrative. Watch for single-name dealer gamma in NVDA, AAPL, TSLA — if those bounce hard, NDX follows.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is too mixed (conflicting inflation/Fed hawkishness vs. geopolitical relief) to warrant aggressive directional entry. Wait for NFP Friday or for one of the competing narratives to dominate; for now, consider small NEUTRAL hedge (straddle) or wait for NDX to test 30,200 support or break 30,700 resistance with conviction before entering. Avoid chasing into Friday's event risk.

factors.name: Broadcom weak outlook / AI capex reality check
factors.summary: Broadcom's weak forward guidance sparked fears that the AI-fueled rally has overextended. This directly challenges the primary bull narrative for Nasdaq 100 (NVDA, semiconductor complex). Michael Saylor attributes BTC decline to 'capital rotation into AI infrastructure,' suggesting some profit-taking in speculative assets.

factors.name: Dow outperformance & sector rotation
factors.summary: Dow surged +1.84% (+930 pts) to record highs led by Financials (JPM +3.64%, GS +4.93%), while Nasdaq components underperformed. This is classic risk-off for mega-cap growth: investors rotating OUT of high-multiple tech INTO value/financials.

factors.name: Fed hawkish messaging (Schmid, Daly)
factors.summary: Kansas City Fed Schmid explicitly called inflation 'the biggest risk facing the economy' and suggested rates may need to stay elevated or tighten further. Fed's Daly echoed inflation priority. This contradicts dovish expectations and weighs on long-duration growth valuations (NQ's core holding).

factors.name: Oil & commodity volatility easing geopolitical premium
factors.summary: WTI slid 3% on Israel-Lebanon ceasefire hopes and Trump's reported reluctance to restart Iran war. Lower energy prices ease inflation fears (gold +1%, EUR gaining), but this also removes a safe-haven bid that had supported risk-off trading. Mixed directional effect.

factors.name: Real yields elevated, duration headwind
factors.summary: 10-year UST yield oscillated around 4.687% (up 76 bps since early March). Elevated real yields directly compress Nasdaq's high-multiple growth stocks. No Fed pivot signals or inflation break evident in recent data.

factors.name: Crypto capitulation & BTC weakness
factors.summary: Bitcoin fell to $62K (down 13% this week, near $60K mining cost floor), erasing $2T in market cap. BTC-NQ correlation remains positive on risk-off days; weakness in speculative assets often precedes or accompanies tech selloffs.

factors.name: Treasury Secretary Bessent market-timing comment
factors.summary: Bessent said 'I wish job market had come out today' ahead of Friday NFP release, fueling concerns about insider trading. Market anxiety over data surprises and political interference can spike volatility into major releases (NFP tomorrow).

factors.name: IPO activity & capital allocation
factors.summary: Quantinuum +13% IPO pop, Liftoff +21%, Keystone $287.5M IPO, and SpaceX IPO announced. While IPO strength suggests risk appetite, it also signals capital being diverted from secondary equity buying into primary offerings.

factors.name: Long-term unemployment surge & labor market softening signals
factors.summary: Reports highlight rising long-term unemployment and labor market cooling. Fed sees 'hard to say labor market has firmed,' potentially dovish long-term, but contradicts near-term inflation hawks. Data conflicted.

factors.name: No major tech earnings or AI capex guidance imminent
factors.summary: No AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA earnings announced in this window. Without hyperscaler guidance or next-gen capex surprises, momentum lacks a fresh catalyst. Void leaves market vulnerable to macro cross-currents.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a cross-current: the AI rally's fundamentals face reality-check pressure (Broadcom weak outlook, capex doubts), while Fed hawks reiterate inflation priority and long-duration multiples remain compressed by elevated real yields. Simultaneous Dow rotation into Financials and away from mega-cap tech signals a shift in risk appetite—investors are taking profits in high-flying Nasdaq constituents and rotating into cyclicals. Bitcoin's 13% weekly dive mirrors the sentiment. However, NQ's +0.42% 24h gain and -0.15% 4h action suggest the market is still digesting rather than in a sharp reversal; this is early-to-mid phase 2, with no clear directional commitment yet.
catalysts_ahead: NFP (May payrolls) – Friday 8:30 AM ET – HIGH IMPACT (jobs data will reset Fed rate-cut odds and tech valuation multiples)
Treasury auction schedule & 10Y yield guidance – could reset real yield expectations
FOMC meeting minutes (if released in window) – any dovish surprise would spark tech rally
Mega-cap tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL) – likely next 2-4 weeks; hyperscaler capex guidance critical
CPI/PCE print (if due in window) – inflation data will dominate NQ direction
screening.risk_warning: If NFP comes in HOT (beats estimates), Fed remains hawkish, and real yields spike, NAS100 could break 30000 support on acceleration. Conversely, if NFP DISAPPOINTS and signals labor market softening, Fed could pivot dovish and spark a 2-3% relief rally into tech. Current setup is bifurcated: Broadcom weakness + Fed hawkishness argue SELL, but stretched move lower + potential for dovish reversal argue CAUTION. Avoid chasing either direction into Friday NFP.
screening.entry_guidance: Do not chase. NQ is in early reversion phase after Broadcom shock. Wait for either: (1) NFP-driven clarity (if dovish, buy dips to 30200 support); (2) retest of 30600 resistance with volume (potential short setup); (3) Fed commentary pivot or inflation surprise. Current 5/10 confidence reflects conflicting signals. Stay in observer mode until one narrative (tech weakness vs. dovish pivot) wins clearly.
